title: PEARL trial: first long-term rapamycin RCT in humans shows muscle and bone benefits
description: The landmark Participatory Evaluation of Aging with Rapamycin for Longevity (PEARL) trial in 50-85 year-olds reported no serious adverse events at 48 weeks, with 10 mg weekly preserving muscle mass in women and bone mineral content in men. This is the first long-term human RCT supporting rapamycin as a healthspan-extending intervention in older adults.

title: Bar-Ilan SIRT6 boost reverses age-related liver decline in mice
description: A Bar-Ilan, Tel Aviv University and NIH on Aging team report that elevating SIRT6 in aged mouse livers reverses metabolic, fibrotic and gene-expression markers of aging. The work, anchored in Haim Cohen's SirTLab spin-out, sets up small-molecule SIRT6 activators as a translational geroprotector class.

title: UT Health San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin healthspan trial
description: UT San Antonio researchers led by Dr. David Kellogg are enrolling non-diabetic 65-90 year-olds in a multi-phase clinical study to map rapamycin's effects on arterial stiffness, cardiometabolic biomarkers and functional aging. Combined with PEARL, this brings two large US rapamycin healthspan trials online simultaneously.

title: Brad Stanfield rapamycin-plus-exercise trial reveals mixed but informative results
description: Dr. Brad Stanfield's New Zealand RCT in 40 adults over 65 tested 6 mg/week rapamycin with exercise. Headline endpoints did not all clear placebo, but the data clarify dosing, autophagy-dependence and the need for higher doses in older muscle - a key step in translating rapamycin science to protocols.

title: Biotech ETFs sell off 1.5-3% Monday as M&A megadeals reset valuations
description: Despite Lilly's three vaccine acquisitions (combined up to $3.83B) and Pfizer-Innovent's $10.5B oncology pact, longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs sold off Monday: XBI -2.25%, SBIO -2.99%, IBB -1.63%. The reset coincides with HCMIS 2026 Day 1 in Columbus highlighting longevity as a growing investable category.

title: PEARL rapamycin RCT published: first long-term human safety and efficacy data
description: PEARL reports no serious adverse events across 48 weeks of weekly rapamycin in older adults, with 10 mg weekly preserving muscle in women and bone in men. Authors flag this as the first long-term anti-aging human RCT for an mTOR inhibitor.

title: Brad Stanfield rapamycin-plus-exercise trial: shocking but informative results
description: Dr. Brad Stanfield walks through his RCT of 6 mg/week rapamycin in adults over 65 combined with exercise - mixed primary endpoint but useful muscle-inflammation and autophagy signals.

title: UT Health San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin clinical study
description: Dr. David Kellogg's team at UT Health San Antonio is enrolling non-diabetic 65-90 year-olds in a multi-phase rapamycin healthspan trial mapping vascular, metabolic and functional endpoints.

title: Long-term rapamycin suppresses pathogenic IL-17 producing T cells: PLOS ONE
description: A PLOS ONE paper shows that chronic low-dose rapamycin suppresses pro-inflammatory IL-17 producing T cells, supporting an immunomodulatory mechanism for healthspan extension.

title: Bar-Ilan team reverses age-related decline in mouse livers via SIRT6
description: Bar-Ilan, Tel Aviv University and NIA researchers report that boosting SIRT6 expression in aged mouse livers reverses key signs of decline, validating SIRT6 as a longevity drug target via the spin-out SirTLab.

title: WSJ: Putin's $26B Kremlin-backed longevity program revealed
description: The Wall Street Journal details Russia's emerging $26B Kremlin-backed program around organ replacement and longevity therapeutics, including the Beijing hot-mic exchange between Putin and Xi.

title: ScienceDaily warning: popular anti-aging drug combo caused severe brain side effects
description: A new study flags severe neurological side effects from a widely used anti-aging drug combination, prompting calls for biomarker-guided dosing and tighter post-market surveillance.

title: HCMIS 2026 Day 1: 'Longevity in Healthcare & Capital Investment' headlines summit
description: Day 1 of HCMIS 2026 in Columbus, Ohio framed longevity as an emerging investment opportunity, with panels on preventive care, AI, GLP-1s and personalized medicine for healthspan and lifespan.

title: Lilly acquires three private vaccine companies for up to $3.83B combined
description: Eli Lilly announced agreements to acquire Curevo ($1.5B), LimmaTech ($780M) and Vaccine Company Inc. ($1.55B) in a single week, advancing a Phase 3-ready shingles vaccine and broader adult immunization platform.

title: Pfizer + Innovent ink $10.5B 12-program oncology pact
description: Pfizer and Suzhou-based Innovent Biologics signed a strategic global licensing pact across 12 early-stage cancer programs valued at up to $10.5B, with $650M upfront and double-digit royalties.

title: ClearNote Health closes $52M Series D for blood-based early detection
description: ClearNote Health, building multiomic blood-based early detection tests for pancreatic and ovarian cancer, closed a $52M Series D led by founding investor Mattias Westman, bringing total funding past $185M.

title: Longevity Show 2026 unveils five pitch competition finalists
description: Anyo Labs, Enbiosis Biotechnology, OvartiX, The Protein Brewery and Zuri Health were chosen from 30 entrants to pitch at The Longevity Show 2026 in London (Tobacco Dock, 26-27 June 2026).

title: Peter Attia: lowering the barrier to entry for resistance training
description: Attia synthesizes a new meta-analysis showing LM2 (low-load, multiple sets, 2x/week) captures most strength, hypertrophy and mobility benefits in untrained adults - a 'minimum effective protocol' for sarcopenia prevention.

title: Bryan Johnson Blueprint 2026 supplement stack walkthrough
description: Comprehensive walkthrough of Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint stack with dosages: NMN/NR, Ca-AKG, spermidine, fisetin, resveratrol, NDGA 50 mg, CoQ10, and timing across the day.

title: Social Life Magazine: Bryan Johnson launches 'Immortals' at $1M/year, 3 spots
description: Profile of Bryan Johnson's Project Blueprint and the 'Immortals' offering ($1M/year, 3 spots) bundling concierge care, BryanAI 24/7 guidance and continuous biomarker tracking.

title: Peter Attia Outlive: where Medicine 3.0 stands in 2026
description: Long-form on Peter Attia's Medicine 3.0 framework, Early Medical practice and Biograph membership tiers (up to $14,500/yr Black), and the 2026 DoJ Epstein-files context.

title: Aging May One Day Be Treated From the Cell Up: senolytics primer
description: Engineering Post primer on senolytics and senescent 'zombie cell' clearance, summarizing mid-2026 mainstream understanding of the senolytic class - now widely covered in consumer media.

title: UNT Health Science Center wins NIH grant for retinal-scan early Alzheimer's detection
description: The HSC Institute for Translational Research received an NIH grant to develop retinal scanning to detect early-stage Alzheimer's, opening a cheap, scalable longevity biomarker pathway.

title: Outside Magazine: longevity and biohacking industry valued at $30B
description: Outside Magazine sizes the global longevity and biohacking market at $30B in 2026, mapping the major players across supplements, diagnostics, clinics and protocols.

title: Wikipedia timeline of aging research updates 2026 milestones
description: The Timeline of Aging Research adds 2026 milestones including senolytic vaccine prototypes, Klotho-mediated dasatinib+quercetin mechanism, and Sinclair lab's chemical-reprogramming chemistry.

title: Fight Aging June 1 newsletter: ProAL50 allostatic-load index leads digest
description: This week's Fight Aging digest leads with the ProAL50 proteomic allostatic-load index, multi-omics clock convergence, nanomaterials in osteoarthritis cartilage and exceptional-longevity genetics.

title: Mass General Brigham thymus Nature paper continues to ripple through aging press
description: Follow-up commentary continues on MGB's Nature paper showing AI-scored thymic fat ratio on chest CT predicts mortality, cardiovascular death and cancer outcomes - a new candidate aging biomarker.

title: ASCO 2026 Galleri MCED first RCT readout enters longevity discussion
description: NHS-Galleri's first-RCT readout (14% reduction in stage IV diagnoses, 19% increase in stage I-III over 3 years) continues to be debated as a longevity-relevant preventive screening tool.

title: HAYA Therapeutics HTX-001 first cohort dosed for cardiac fibrosis
description: HAYA Therapeutics' WISPER-targeting antisense oligonucleotide HTX-001 enters Phase 1 in cardiac fibrosis, a key longevity-aligned mechanism of death in older adults.

title: Olympus acquires BioProtect for $270M, expands urology-oncology portfolio
description: Olympus Corporation closed a $270M deal for Israeli MedTech BioProtect, adding a prostate-cancer radiation spacer device that improves outcomes for older men with localized disease.
